title: Email a Teacher a Question
summary: Write a polite, clear email to a teacher asking a specific question.
learnerPromise: You will be able to write a short, respectful email to a teacher that clearly states your question and includes the information they need to help you.
whyItMatters: Email is a common way to communicate with teachers outside of class. A clear and polite email helps you get a faster, more useful response.
realLifeUses: Asking a teacher about a homework question you could not solve
Requesting clarification on an assignment due date
Informing a teacher that you will miss class and need to know what you missed
Following up on a conversation you had with a teacher after class
materialsNeeded: Email account or school messaging system
Your class schedule or assignment details (to include in the email)
result: A sent email to a teacher with a clear, polite question
youWillLearn: How to start an email with a polite greeting using the teacher's name
How to state your question in one or two clear sentences
How to include important details like class period or assignment name
How to end an email politely with your name
miniChallenge: Write and send an email to a teacher with a specific question about a class, assignment, or due date. Keep it to 3-5 sentences.
steps: Start with a greeting like 'Dear Ms. Smith,' or 'Hello Mr. Lee,'.
In the first sentence, say who you are and which class you are in.
State your question or request clearly in one or two sentences.
Include any helpful details like the assignment name or page number.
End with a polite closing like 'Thank you' and sign your full name.
completionCriteria: Email includes a greeting, your name, class, and a specific question
Message is 3-5 sentences and uses a polite tone throughout
You receive a reply or confirmation that the email was sent successfully
commonProblems: Writing a vague message like 'I need help' without saying what the problem is
Forgetting to include your name or class so the teacher knows who you are
Using texting shortcuts or informal language in a school email
tips: Use the teacher's last name unless they have told you to use their first name
Reread your email before sending to check for typos
If it is urgent, mention a deadline, but give the teacher at least a day to reply
